title: Quantity surveyors
description: Apply knowledge of engineering, biology, chemistry, computer science, and biomechanical principles to the design, development, and evaluation of biological, agricultural, and health systems and products, such as artificial organs, prostheses, instrumentation, medical information systems, and health management and care delivery systems. Promote worksite or product safety by applying knowledge of industrial processes, mechanics, chemistry, psychology, and industrial health and safety laws. Includes industrial product safety engineers. Research causes of fires, determine fire protection methods, and design or recommend materials or equipment such as structural components or fire-detection equipment to assist organizations in safeguarding life and property against fire, explosion, and related hazards. (Based on 32 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Testify in court about investigative or analytical methods or findings.
Monitor equipment during operation, ensuring adherence to specifications for characteristics such as pressure, temperature, or flow.
Maintain records and logbooks, including numerical data, test results, photographs, or summaries of visual observations.
Inspect fire hazards in forest tracts and recommend fire prevention measures, while also inspecting camp sites to ensure compliance with regulations.
Administer regulations regarding sanitation, fire prevention, and violation corrections in forest areas.
Estimate sizes and characteristics of fires and report findings to base camps by communication devices.
Direct maintenance and repair of firefighting equipment, including requisitioning new equipment as necessary.
Collect or compile data related to pollution or contamination to ensure compliance with environmental quality standards.
Educate the public about fire safety and prevention, including conducting wildland firefighting training.
Perform inspections and monitor environments for compliance with health and safety regulations including safety tags on equipment needing maintenance.
Compile and evaluate statistical data to assess the quality and reliability of products or processes, and prepare reports detailing findings and methods.
Identify sources and analyze contaminants or pollutants through sampling of air, water, soil, or other materials.
Assist in planning and implementing improvements in production or quality assurance programs, while also overseeing production processes.
Develop recommendations for correcting or preventing quality issues in production processes or materials based on data analysis.
Prepare various training materials and documentation to transfer processes to production, ensuring compliance with specifications and safety standards.
Locate and monitor forest fires using established methods, including responding to emergencies.
Monitor hazardous waste cleanup procedures to ensure adherence to safety and environmental standards.
Collect evidence from crime scenes and perform investigations related to forensic analysis, including analyzing patterns and impressions.
Inspect and measure equipment and environmental safety conditions to maintain operational compliance and performance standards.
Conduct training or provide instruction on safety procedures and operational compliance to workers and personnel.
Collaborate with other professionals to design or conduct experiments, ensuring projects and research are aligned with safety and regulatory guidelines.
Prepare and review documentation to verify compliance with environmental permits and safety regulations.
Analyze and interpret data related to environmental conditions or contamination levels to inform safety and compliance decisions.
Analyze life cycles of materials or processes to assess environmental impacts and improve sustainability measures.
Develop and implement programs or methods to address production, quality, or safety issues, while maximizing efficiency and minimizing waste.
Prepare production layouts or drawings for new equipment installations based on engineering principles and safety requirements.
Calibrate and maintain equipment accuracy for safe and effective operation in compliance with health and safety protocols.
Assist engineers in prototype testing or product development while adhering to industry standards and regulations.
Create models or simulations to demonstrate processes or predict environmental impacts in regulated activities.
Assist in public meetings or consultations regarding environmental impacts of proposed projects or safety concerns in forest areas.
Ensure environmental compliance during all phases of operations, including overseeing hazardous material handling and disposal procedures.
Manage the required safety checks and maintain proper records for hazardous materials and operational compliance.

Measure the intensity and identify the types of radiation in work areas, equipment, or materials, using radiation detectors or other instruments, and warn maintenance workers of radiation hazards while directing workers to vacate hazardous areas.
Calculate equipment operating factors, such as radiation times, dosages, temperatures, gamma intensities, or pressures, using standard formulas and conversion tables.
Perform testing, maintenance, repair, or upgrading of accelerator systems, and operate and maintain laboratory equipment and apparatus.

Collect samples of air, water, gases, or solids to determine radioactivity levels of contamination, and prepare reports describing contamination tests, materials or equipment decontaminated, or methods used in decontamination processes.
Follow policies and procedures for radiation workers to ensure personnel safety, and ensure that lab cleanliness and safety standards are maintained.
Set up equipment that automatically detects area radiation deviations and test detection equipment to ensure its accuracy.
Determine or recommend radioactive decontamination procedures based on the size and nature of equipment and the degree of contamination, and decontaminate objects by cleaning or abrading surfaces.
Evaluate potential health hazards or damage that could occur from product misuse, and investigate industrial accidents, injuries, or occupational diseases to determine causes and preventive measures.

Conduct or coordinate worker training in areas such as safety laws and regulations, hazardous condition monitoring, and use of safety equipment, while reviewing employee safety programs to determine their adequacy.

Maintain and apply knowledge of current policies, regulations, and industrial processes, while reading current literature and participating in professional organizations to stay abreast of developments in the field.

Write or revise standard quality control operating procedures, and prepare documentation including technical reports or articles for scientific publication, regulatory submissions, or patent applications.

Conduct routine analyses of in-process materials, raw materials, environmental samples, finished goods, or stability samples, and compile, analyze, and interpret statistical data related to occupational illnesses and accidents.
Compile laboratory test data and perform appropriate analyses, while evaluating analytical methods to determine potential improvements.
Evaluate new technologies and methods to make recommendations regarding their use, including the development of quality strategies based on testing and analysis.

Write documents describing protocols, policies, standards for use, maintenance, and repair of medical equipment, and develop methodologies for transferring procedures from laboratories to commercial-scale production.
Monitor testing procedures to ensure that all tests are performed according to established item specifications, and participate in internal assessments and audits as required.
Collaborate with other departments, vendors, or contractors to provide expert knowledge, while serving as a technical liaison.
Review plans and specifications for construction of new machinery or equipment to ensure safety requirements are met, and interpret safety regulations for others interested in industrial safety.
Determine the feasibility, costs, or performance benefits of new equipment, and create mechanical models to simulate design concepts.
Conduct environmental studies on nuclear-related activities and prepare environmental impact statements for regulatory agencies.
Review existing manufacturing processes to identify opportunities for yield improvement or reduced process variation.
Direct environmental compliance activities associated with nuclear plant operations or maintenance, and recommend procedures for the prevention and elimination of physical hazards.
Identify quality problems and recommend solutions based on quality control data.
Research renewable or alternative energy systems or technologies, including solar thermal or photovoltaic energy, and promote awareness or use of such energy sources.
Direct the implementation of energy management projects and manage the development, design, or construction of energy conservation projects to ensure compliance with budgets and timelines.
Prepare energy-related project reports or related documentation, including technical reports of findings or recommendations based on analyses of test results.
Review architectural, mechanical, or electrical plans or specifications to evaluate energy efficiency, and identify and recommend energy savings strategies for more efficient operations.
Collect data for energy conservation analyses, conduct energy audits to evaluate energy use, and verify energy bills and meter readings.
Advise clients or colleagues on climate control systems, energy modeling, data logging, sustainable design, and energy auditing activities.
Monitor energy-related design or construction issues, including energy engineering and sustainable design, and conduct analyses to address issues such as reliability or yield improvement.

Conduct research, along with scientists, on the engineering aspects of biological systems of humans and animals, and integrate findings into medical or clinical instrumentation, equipment, or procedures.
Design or develop medical diagnostic or clinical instrumentation based on principles of engineering and biobehavioral sciences, ensuring safety and efficacy of biomedical equipment.
Design advanced precision equipment for controlled applications, including design and oversight of fire detection equipment, alarm systems, and fire extinguishing systems.
Conduct research on fire retardants, analyze the causes of fires, and evaluate fire department performance, laws, and regulations affecting fire prevention or fire safety.
Develop training materials and conduct training sessions on topics related to fire protection and safety, including compliance with fire codes and standards interpretation.
Communicate with suppliers and authorities regarding the design and specifications of bioproduction equipment and fire protection equipment, ensuring compliance with safety regulations.
Inspect buildings to determine fire protection system requirements and to identify potential problems in construction materials, exit locations, or water supplies.
Perform computer simulation of solar energy systems, assess performance metrics, and establish technical design specifications and requirements for solar power systems.
Create plans for solar energy system development, monitoring, and evaluation activities, including detailed diagrams and checklists for completed installations.
Evaluate the safety, efficiency, and effectiveness of nuclear technologies, and design or oversee construction of nuclear equipment or power plants, ensuring adherence to safety standards.
Initiate corrective actions or plant shutdowns in emergency situations, based on data from monitoring operations and evaluations of performance test results.
Conduct tests of nuclear fuel behavior and cycles or nuclear machinery and equipment to optimize the performance of existing plants.
Keep abreast of developments and changes in nuclear technology by engaging with technical journals and independent research.
Oversee the operation of microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) fabrication equipment to ensure compliance and effectiveness in production processes.
Develop or validate product-specific test protocols or acceptance thresholds for quality control testing of MEMS devices and applications.
Create or maintain formal engineering documents, such as schematics and specifications for energy efficiency projects and fire protection systems.
Communicate operating characteristics or performance experiences to other engineers for the purpose of training or new product development in energy or fire protection technologies.
Conduct harsh environmental testing to validate equipment performance and ensure design meets safety and operational requirements for systems related to energy or fire safety.
Consult with construction or renovation clients or other engineers on topics such as Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design (LEED) or Green Buildings.
Inspect or monitor energy systems, including heating, ventilating, and air conditioning (HVAC) or daylighting systems to determine energy use or potential energy savings.
Monitor and analyze energy consumption.
Develop standard operation procedures and quality or safety standards for solar installation work.
Analyze, interpret, or create graphical representations of energy data, using engineering software.
Review or negotiate energy purchase agreements.
Train personnel or clients on topics such as energy management.
Test or evaluate photovoltaic (PV) cells or modules.
Analyze system performance or operational requirements.
Review building plans to verify compliance with fire code.
Perform thermal, stress, or cost reduction analyses for solar systems.
Review specifications and recommend engineering or manufacturing changes to achieve solar design objectives.
Create models to optimize the layout of wind farm access roads, crane pads, crane paths, collection systems, substations, switchyards, or transmission lines.
Conduct research related to a range of nanotechnology topics, such as packaging, heat transfer, and nanocomposites.
Create designs or prototypes for nanosystem applications, such as biomedical delivery systems or atomic force microscopes.
Engineer production processes for specific nanotechnology applications, such as electroplating, nanofabrication, or epoxy.
Evaluate technical specifications and economic factors relating to process or product design objectives.
Conduct fire exit drills to monitor and evaluate evacuation procedures.
Review blueprints and plans for new or remodeled buildings to ensure the structures meet fire safety codes.
Inspect properties that store, handle, and use hazardous materials to ensure compliance with laws, codes, and regulations, and issue hazardous materials permits to facilities found in compliance.
Develop or review fire exit plans.
Inspect and test fire protection or fire detection systems to verify that such systems are installed in accordance with appropriate laws, codes, ordinances, regulations, and standards.
Recommend changes to fire prevention, inspection, and fire code endorsement procedures.
Prepare and maintain reports of investigation results, and records of convicted arsonists and arson suspects.
Monitor material performance, and evaluate its deterioration.
Develop specifications for wind technology components, such as gearboxes, blades, generators, or frequency converters.
Supervise and participate in the inspection of properties to ensure that they are in compliance with applicable fire codes, ordinances, laws, regulations, and standards.
Conduct inspections and acceptance testing of newly installed fire protection systems.
Photograph damage and evidence related to causes of fires or explosions to document investigation findings.
Analyze evidence and other information to determine the probable cause of fire or explosion.
Examine fire sites and collect evidence such as glass, metal fragments, charred wood, and accelerant residue for use in determining the cause of a fire.

Identify corrective actions necessary to bring properties into compliance with applicable fire codes, laws, regulations, and standards, and explain these measures to property owners or their representatives.
Evaluate fire station procedures to ensure efficiency and enforcement of departmental regulations.
Direct investigation of cases of suspected arson, hazards, and false alarms and submit reports outlining findings.
Teach public education programs on fire safety and prevention.
Conduct or supervise tests on raw materials or finished products to ensure their quality.
Supervise production and testing processes in industrial settings, such as metal refining facilities, smelting or foundry operations, or nonmetallic materials production operations.
Write detailed reports of fire inspections performed, fire code violations observed, and corrective recommendations offered.

Conduct inspections and tests on materials, structures, or components to detect defects such as corrosion, cracks, or flaws, using various techniques including visual examinations, ultrasonic testing, and liquid penetrant tests. Document test methods and results, and prepare reports to inform engineering decisions.

Supervise or train staff in non-destructive testing methods and procedures, ensuring adherence to safety standards and operational protocols. Assist engineers in the development of new products, tools, or processes related to non-destructive testing.

Set up and operate testing or assembly equipment, such as cameras or lasers, and conduct tests to ensure the integrity and performance of materials, components, or systems. Optimize equipment settings and parameters to achieve efficient production or testing outcomes.

Assess the nature and extent of defects, evaluate material properties, and recommend changes to designs or processes to enhance safety and efficiency. Maintain clean working environments and monitor inventory levels for supplies.
Produce images of objects using radiographic techniques and interpret the results of tests to identify and analyze defects.
Develop new testing methods or improve existing techniques for non-destructive evaluation of materials, such as acoustic emission or thermal testing.
Assemble components related to optical communications or photonics, ensuring proper calibration and functionality. Fabricate and repair optoelectronic devices or systems as needed.
Document procedures and processes related to non-destructive testing and maintain equipment used in such testing to ensure its readiness and accuracy.
